last_item: install/upgrade — MajorUpgradePreflight (internal/pg/preflight.go), the gate for a DESTRUCTIVE major PG upgrade, had ZERO tests on its runner-driven psql-scraping substrate: scalarCount (backs the prepared-xacts + logical-slot blockers — n>0 blocks) and installedExtensions->listDatabaseNames (backs the extension-parity blocker + preview). Both must FAIL LOUD, not return a clean-looking 0/partial that lets pg_upgrade proceed into a state it can't handle (band-2, Mode-A prove-it).
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): +6 tests in internal/pg/preflight_test.go driving the real Manager methods via &Manager{runner: FakeRunner}. scalarCount: parses 0/3/whitespace-padded; FAILS LOUD (error, not silent (0,nil)) on empty/blank/garbage (a swallowed parse error defeats BOTH blockers at once); propagates psql err. installedExtensions: unions+dedups across DBs, drops blank lines, sorts, and FAILS LOUD (error naming the DB, not a partial set) on a per-DB query error or a db-enumeration error (a partial set under-reports parity -> misses a missing-build blocker). The FS-reading preflight checks (fileExists(pgUpgradeBin), freeBytes, DataDirectory/du) need FS seams -> full end-to-end MajorUpgradePreflight still out of scope, same residual as InstallPreflight Iter #12. Mutation-proven over 8 mutations (perl apply + git checkout, tree clean each): scalarCount parse-err->(0,nil); drop TrimSpace; scalarCount runPsql-err->(0,nil); installedExtensions per-db-err->continue; drop blank-line !="" guard (anchored on seen[line] — the SAME guard line also lives in listDatabaseNames, so a naive perl hits the wrong copy: target the second occurrence); drop sort.Strings (RED x5, probabilistic via map-order); swallow listDatabaseNames err. test-skeptic found a REAL escape: FakeRunner matches the -d app -c argv substring, decoupled from SQL text, so dropping/inverting WHERE extname <> 'plpgsql' stayed GREEN (invert to = 'plpgsql' hides every real ext from the parity blocker). CLOSED before commit by pinning the exclusion predicate on fake.Calls() argv + a one-query-per-database count; re-mutated drop-WHERE/invert/skip-a-database all now RED. code-reviewer clean (matchers unambiguous: -d postgres vs -d app vs -d shop never collide; sort assertion deterministic since source sorts before return). Backend gate green: gofmt clean, vet OK, go test ./... all pass, static CGO_ENABLED=0 build (cmd/indiepg) OK. Web untouched. e2e N/A (pure unit + Docker unavailable).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
